name: Cueport
subtitle: A local-first music player for DJs
description: Start on your Mac, where Cueport reads your Serato library. Pair the iPhone to the Mac over your local network, send some crates across, and play them anywhere. The Mac app is the source of truth and the iPhone app pairs to it.

shipNotes.title: Cueport 1.0: local-first library management for desktop and phone
included: Read Serato crates, subcrates, tracks, cues, BPM, key, color, comments, and beatgrid markers from your local library
Play tracks locally from the desktop library with waveform context, cue markers, and continuous playback
Create, rename, delete, add tracks to, and manually reorder collections before publishing them back
Edit cue points, track comments, and track colors in a local working layer before any write-back happens
Review pending changes before publishing to Serato, with restore points for every publish
Pair an iPhone over the local network, choose collections for the phone, cache audio, and play offline
Use desktop and mobile column sorting as temporary views while keeping the # column as the manual order
Recover from pairing changes without deleting cached phone music
Signed Mac app build with local-first behavior and no music upload requirement
